topic: How millionaires cut obligations to focus on what matters
title: The One Thing Millionaires Cut From Their Schedule

scenes.setting: Modern coffee shop with wooden tables, morning light
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them.
scenes.spoken_text: Millionaires cut obligations, not tasks. I surveyed 100 people earning over a million a year.

scenes.setting: Minimalist home office with a single monitor and bookshelf
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them.
scenes.spoken_text: Every single one said they stopped saying yes to things they didn't want to do.

scenes.setting: Park bench under a tree, natural afternoon light
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them.
scenes.spoken_text: Not busywork. Not tasks. Obligations. The guilt-driven yes that drains your energy.

scenes.setting: Quiet corner of a library with warm lighting
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them.
scenes.spoken_text: That's the only pattern. So ask yourself—what obligation are you keeping that makes you poor? Millionaires cut obligations, not tasks.
